label: HubSpot
description: Read and write contacts, companies, deals, and tickets in your HubSpot portal.

form.title: Connect HubSpot
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read and update your HubSpot CRM.

methods.label: Private app token
methods.description: Paste a Private App access token from HubSpot. Simplest path — no OAuth dance, the token already encodes the scopes you grant when you create it.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a HubSpot Private App access token

Private Apps replaced HubSpot's classic API keys in 2022. They're per-portal, scoped, and revocable from HubSpot's UI in two clicks.

### Steps

1. Sign in to your HubSpot portal as a user with **Super admin** rights.
2. Open **Settings → Integrations → Private Apps** (in the left sidebar of the settings panel).
3. Click **Create a private app** at the top right.
4. On the **Basic Info** tab, name it `Anton` (or whatever helps you recognize it later) and add a short description.
5. Switch to the **Scopes** tab and check the scopes Anton will need. A reasonable starter set:
   - `crm.objects.contacts.read` / `crm.objects.contacts.write`
   - `crm.objects.companies.read` / `crm.objects.companies.write`
   - `crm.objects.deals.read` / `crm.objects.deals.write`
   - `tickets` (if you use tickets)
   - `crm.lists.read` / `crm.lists.write`
6. Click **Create app** at the top right. HubSpot shows the access token (`pat-na1-…` or `pat-eu1-…`).
7. Copy the token and paste it into the field below. Treat it like a password — anyone with it can read/write everything in the scopes you granted.

### Notes

- You can come back any time to add or remove scopes, or rotate the token.
- Anton stores the token locally in the data vault; it never leaves your machine except as `Authorization: Bearer …` headers to `api.hubapi.com`.
- Read [HubSpot's Private Apps guide](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/api/private-apps) for the full background.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Private app access token
fields.description: Starts with pat-na1- (US portals) or pat-eu1- (EU portals).

methods.label: Sign in with HubSpot
methods.description: OAuth 2.0 — a browser pops open for you to grant Anton scoped access. Best for shared workspaces; revocable from each user's HubSpot account. Currently requires you to register a developer app in HubSpot's developer portal (steps below); we'll switch to a hosted client in a future release.
methods.how_to:
## Sign in with HubSpot (OAuth 2.0)

OAuth is the right choice when multiple users in your portal want to use Anton, or when you want to scope access per-user rather than per-portal.

### Pattern A — using Anton's hosted OAuth client

When the connector spec ships with a `client_id`, you don't see any fields here. Click **Submit**, your browser opens HubSpot's consent screen, you pick the portal, click **Connect app**, and the browser hands an authorization code back to Anton through a `127.0.0.1` redirect. Anton exchanges it for a refresh token and stores it locally.

### Pattern B — bring your own developer app

If this method shows two fields below (**Client ID** and **Client secret**), Anton doesn't ship a hosted OAuth client for HubSpot yet — register your own:

1. Open the [HubSpot Developer Portal](https://developers.hubspot.com/) (or create a developer account if you don't have one).
2. **Apps → Create app**. Name it `Anton` (or anything).
3. On the app's **Auth** tab, set the **Redirect URL** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton picks a free loopback port at runtime; HubSpot's redirect-URL match is by host only, port doesn't matter).
4. Add the scopes you want (mirror the recommended Private-App scope list above).
5. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client secret** from the Auth tab into the fields below.
6. Click **Submit**. Anton runs the same browser flow with your client.

### Revoking later

Any portal user can revoke at **HubSpot → Settings → Integrations → Connected Apps**. The next task that hits HubSpot will fail with an auth error and prompt you to re-authorize.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth client ID
fields.description: From your HubSpot developer app → Auth tab.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth client secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID; same Auth tab.
